explanation.rule: Negative prefixes are added to the beginning of words to create opposite or negative meanings.

groups.note: The prefix in- is added to adjectives and some nouns to create a negative or opposite meaning. It is used before most consonants. Note: it changes form before certain letters — im- before b/m/p, ir- before r, il- before l.

examples.definition: not enough or not good enough
not confident; not safe or stable

wordForms.definition: not enough or not good enough for a purpose
wordForms.example: The safety measures were completely inadequate.
He seemed incapable of making a decision under pressure.
wordForms.rootDefinition: having the ability or skill to do something

wordForms.example: The new timetable was very inconvenient for commuters.
wordForms.rootDefinition: fitting well with someone's needs or plans

wordForms.definition: not relying on others; free from control
wordForms.example: She became financially independent after landing her first job.
wordForms.rootDefinition: relying on someone or something for support

wordForms.definition: unable to make decisions quickly or confidently
wordForms.example: His indecisive manner frustrated the rest of the team.

wordForms.definition: impossible to destroy or damage permanently
wordForms.example: This material is virtually indestructible under normal conditions.
wordForms.rootDefinition: able to be broken down or permanently damaged

wordForms.definition: relaxed, casual, and not following official rules
wordForms.example: The meeting was held in a relaxed, informal atmosphere.

wordForms.definition: lacking confidence; not safe or stable
wordForms.example: She felt insecure about her performance during the interview.
wordForms.rootDefinition: confident and free from danger or risk

wordForms.definition: not willing to accept views or behaviour that differ from your own
wordForms.example: The organisation condemned the group's intolerant attitudes.
wordForms.rootDefinition: willing to accept behaviour and views different from your own

wordForms.example: The ink used for the document was invisible under normal light.

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: The safety provisions at the site were considered ___.
He seemed ___ of working without close supervision.
The new schedule was ___ for parents with young children.
The referee's ___ style made the game difficult to follow.
The dress code for the event is ___, so wear whatever you like.
Many passengers were ___ to delays on the main line.

transformExercises.sentence: The funding provided was simply ___ for the scale of the project. (ADEQUATE)
Students who work ___ often develop stronger self-management skills. (DEPEND)
The new password system made the files ___ to unauthorised users. (VISIBLE)
She felt ___ in social situations and rarely joined group discussions. (SECURE)
The material used in the vault was virtually ___. (DESTROY)
